title: Clustering & splitting plots
text: sort_by_clustering() reorders rows haplotype sharing (hierarchical clustering):  Options: cluster major haplotype per cell, restrict well-covered targets, choose distance/linkage, cluster specific subset targets:
code: rb$sort_by_clustering() rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE) rb$sort_by_clustering(by_major_allele = TRUE)                 # major allele only rb$sort_by_clustering(coverage_cutoff = 0.90)                 # targets in >= 90% samples rb$sort_by_clustering(dist_method = "manhattan",             # distance + linkage                       hclust_method = "complete") rb$sort_by_clustering(targets = c("Pf01-0145449-0145622"))   # cluster on these targets

title: Clustering & splitting plots
text: fitted hclust stored; pull hclust dendrogram, cut groups. cluster_groups() returns tibble sample -> cluster, relabelled dendrogram order — ready feed band/gap/export helpers metadata sidebar.
code: hc  <- rb$get_hclust()          # the stats::hclust object den <- rb$get_dendrogram()      # as a dendrogram grp <- rb$cluster_groups(k = 6) # sample -> cluster head(grp) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   s_Sample cluster #>   <chr>    <fct>   #> 1 PG0401-C 1       #> 2 PG0400-C 1       #> 3 HB3      1       #> 4 PG0399-C 1       #> 5 PG0414-C 2       #> 6 7G8      2

title: Clustering & splitting plots
text: add_cluster_bands() overlays alternating bands, one per cluster group. dense grid translucent overlay alone subtle, expand pushes bands visible margin strips border draws line around block:  Tune look: opaque colors, wider expand, extend_left/extend_right reach across sidebar add underneath:
code: p <- rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE) rb$add_cluster_bands(p, k = 6, expand = 2, border = "black") p <- rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE) rb$add_cluster_bands(p, k = 4, expand = 4,                      colors = c("#00000060", "#AAAAAA60"), border = "black")

title: Clustering & splitting plots
text: add_cluster_bands() overlay — cells stay flush. real physical gap, add_cluster_gaps() inserts blank spacer rows clusters, cells, sidebar bands separate together (everything positioned sample factor). chains, gap = 0 removes spacers:  gap baked row positions, sidebar cluster bands added top line :
code: rb$sort_by_clustering()$add_cluster_gaps(k = 6, gap = 2) rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE) rb$set_sample_meta(pfIsosHeomeV1_sampleMeta, match_col = "sample", cols = "region") p <- rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE, y_axis_labels = FALSE) p <- rb$add_sample_metadata(p, cols = "region", width = 3, target_labels = FALSE) rb$add_cluster_bands(p, k = 6, expand = 3, colors = c("#00000030", "#AAAAAA30"))

what: A plot per cluster (or metadata group)
title: Clustering & splitting plots
text: export_groups_pdf() renders one plot per group, auto-sizing sample count, combines single multi-page PDF (page correctly sized) via qpdf package. align_targets = TRUE (default) sample names padded common width plot body / targets line position every page. pass plot_fun builds plot per-group sub-object, every page can carry sidebar / annotations:
code: rb$sort_by_clustering() rb$export_groups_pdf(   "clusters.pdf",   plot_fun = function(sub) sub$plot(rank_colors = TRUE),   by = "cluster", k = 6 )  # group by a sample-metadata column instead, writing a separate file per group rb$export_groups_pdf(   "by_region.pdf",   plot_fun = function(sub) {     p <- sub$plot()     sub$add_sample_metadata(p, cols = "country")   },   by = "meta", meta_col = "region", combine = FALSE )

title: Colours & plot styles
text: default dominant haplotype’s hue rotates across targets period (color_period, default 11), produces “rainbow”. shorter period cycles colours faster:  Supply ramp colors (length match color_period):  fill can driven either colour column prep — log-spaced (default) linear hue:
code: rb$prep(sort = "population_rank", color_period = 6) rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE) rb$plot(colors = c("#F50300", "#FF6E00", "#FFEB01", "#00CA1E", "#0241FE", "#FE00D4")) rb$plot(color_col = "popidFracLogColor")   # default (log-spaced hue) rb$plot(color_col = "popidFracRegColor")   # linear hue

title: Colours & plot styles
text: package ships three categorical palettes (colorPalette_08, colorPalette_12, colorPalette_15) used metadata sidebars available use:
code: scales::show_col(colorPalette_12)

title: Colours & plot styles
text: plot(rank_colors = TRUE) colours haplotypes discrete rank legend, abundant haplotype (rank 1) colour every target — flat, easy--read alternative gradient. ranks palette colours, ramp interpolated palette (dropping white/black, ordering hue, reordering consecutive ranks stay distinct — abundant ranks 1, 2, 3 won’t come near-identical). Prep mark_invariant = TRUE give single-haplotype (invariant) targets colour (black default).  get_rank_colors() returns rank -> colour map (e.g. reuse elsewhere); pass different rank_palette plot() / get_rank_colors() change :
code: rb$prep(sort = "population_rank", mark_invariant = TRUE)$sort_by_clustering() rb$plot(rank_colors = TRUE, x_axis_labels = FALSE) head(rb$get_rank_colors(), 5) #>         1         2         3         4         5  #> "#FF6E3A" "#00D5EB" "#D1012F" "#00F5C9" "#A40122"

title: Colours & plot styles
text: Instead rotating rainbow, prep_shade() colours target shades per-target base colour. plot() uses shade colours automatically:  Pass base colours (target cycles , shades within):
code: rb$prep_shade(min_pop_size = 1) rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE) rb$prep_shade(base_colors = colorPalette_08) rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE)

title: Getting started
text: need four columns: sample id, target id, within-target haplotype id, relative count (raw counts fine — ’re normalised within-sample fractions internally). defaults follow Portable Microhaplotype Object (PMO) convention (library_sample_name / target_name / seq / reads); example data uses older SeekDeep names, pass explicitly. haplotype_rainbow() identical convenience constructor PMO-named tables: Chaining. Transforming methods (prep(), prep_shade(), sort_*(), set_sample_order(), add_cluster_gaps()) mutate object return invisibly, chain. plot() add_*() helpers return ggplot.
code: rb <- HaplotypeRainbow$new(   pfIsosHeomeV1,   sample_col    = "s_Sample",   target_col    = "p_name",   popuid_col    = "h_popUID",   rel_abund_col = "c_AveragedFrac" ) rb #> <HaplotypeRainbow> #>   columns: sample = s_Sample | target = p_name | haplotype = h_popUID | counts = c_AveragedFrac  #>   rows in: 7611  #>   prepped: <not yet - call $prep()> rb <- haplotype_rainbow(my_pmo_allele_table)   # PMO column names, no mapping needed rb$prep(sort = "population_rank")$sort_by_clustering()$plot()

title: Getting started
text: prep() must called plotting. sort controls haplotypes stack within cell:   prep() knobs — min_pop_size drops sparse targets, color_period sets rainbow period (see colours article), bar_height < 1 leaves gap sample rows:
code: rb$prep(sort = "population_rank")   # order by population rank (default) rb$plot() rb$prep(sort = "within_sample_freq")   # order by within-sample fraction rb$plot() rb$prep(sort = "population_rank", bar_height = 0.6)   # thinner bars, more row gap rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE)

title: Getting started
text: Target names (x) sample names (y) show default. Toggle either — plot keeps tight margins leftover ticks, handy many samples targets label:
code: rb$prep(sort = "population_rank") rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE)                        # hide target names rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE, y_axis_labels = FALSE) # hide both

title: Getting started
text: get_prepped() returns prepped table want post-process plot inspect derived columns:
code: head(rb$get_prepped()) #> # A tibble: 6 × 22 #>   s_Sample p_name       h_popUID c_AveragedFrac totalAbund s_COI relAbundCol_mod #>   <fct>    <fct>        <chr>             <dbl>      <dbl> <int>           <dbl> #> 1 3D7      Pf01-014544… Pf01-01…              1          1     1             0.8 #> 2 3D7      Pf01-017990… Pf01-01…              1          1     1             0.8 #> 3 3D7      Pf01-018155… Pf01-01…              1          1     1             0.8 #> 4 3D7      Pf01-049597… Pf01-04…              1          1     1             0.8 #> 5 3D7      Pf01-051219… Pf01-05…              1          1     1             0.8 #> 6 3D7      Pf01-053168… Pf01-05…              1          1     1             0.8 #> # ℹ 15 more variables: fracCumSum <dbl>, fracModCumSum <dbl>, fakeFrac <dbl>, #> #   fakeFracMod <dbl>, fakeFracCumSum <dbl>, fakeFracModCumSum <dbl>, #> #   samp_n <int>, popid <int>, maxPopid <int>, popidFrac <dbl>, hueMod <dbl>, #> #   popidPerc <dbl>, popidFracRegColor <dbl>, popidPercLog <dbl>, #> #   popidFracLogColor <dbl>

title: Sample & target metadata
text: set_sample_meta() / set_target_meta() take metadata frame column matches sample / target names: error match_col (requested cols) absent, warn + NA-fill samples/targets metadata row. second call replaces stored metadata default; pass add = TRUE merge extra columns. Restrict particular columns cols:
code: rb$set_sample_meta(pfIsosHeomeV1_sampleMeta, match_col = "sample") rb$set_target_meta(pfIsosHeomeV1_targetMeta, match_col = "target_name") rb$set_sample_meta(pfIsosHeomeV1_sampleMeta, match_col = "sample",                    cols = c("country", "region")) rb$set_sample_meta(more_meta, match_col = "sample", add = TRUE)   # merge, don't replace

title: Sample & target metadata
text: Order rows columns metadata; entities metadata go end:
code: rb$sort_samples_by_meta(c("secondaryRegion", "country")) rb$sort_targets_by_meta("class") rb$set_target_order(c("Pf01-0145449-0145622"))   # or an explicit target order

title: Sample & target metadata
text: add_sample_metadata() draws one coloured band per metadata column left (right) rainbow. Sizes cell units: width/height 1 matches rainbow cell, 2 double, 0.8 80% (centred). Band labels drawn independently sample / target names, can keep target names :  Colours auto-assigned colour-blind palettes. sidebar knobs placement, spacing, — importantly wide metadata — legend layout order: side = "right" moves side. plot_gap separates bands cells (default 1). legend_ncol / legend_nrow reshape big legends (scalar bands, named vector per column) stay page. level_order (named list per column) controls legend entry order; unlisted values appended sorted order. colors overrides auto colours per column; na_color sets missing-value fill; legend = FALSE drops legends.
code: p <- rb$plot(y_axis_labels = FALSE) rb$add_sample_metadata(   p,   cols          = c("secondaryRegion", "region", "country"),   width         = 3,     # 3 cells wide   gap           = 0.5,   # gap between bands   target_labels = FALSE  # hide target names, keep the band labels ) p <- rb$plot(y_axis_labels = FALSE) rb$add_sample_metadata(   p,   cols        = c("region", "country"),   side        = "right",   plot_gap    = 2,                                     # push off the rainbow   legend_ncol = c(country = 4, region = 2),            # fold tall legends   level_order = list(region = c("West Africa", "East Africa")),   target_labels = FALSE )

title: Sample & target metadata
text: add_target_annotation() transpose: coloured bands (position = "top", default) rainbow, one per target-metadata column. Stack multiple columns space gap:  Put strip instead, reshape legend way sidebar:
code: p <- rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE, y_axis_labels = FALSE) p <- rb$add_sample_metadata(p, cols = c("region", "country"), width = 3, gap = 0.5,                             target_labels = FALSE) # two stacked target layers: class (innermost) then chrom rb$add_target_annotation(p, cols = c("class", "chrom"), height = 2, gap = 0.5,                          plot_gap = 1, sample_labels = FALSE) rb$add_target_annotation(p, cols = "chrom", position = "bottom",                          legend_ncol = 2, level_order = list(chrom = c("Pf3D7_01_v3")))

title: Saving, sizing & interactivity
text: dims() scales width number targets height number samples (counting inter-cluster spacer rows), respecting minimums. Use size Quarto / R Markdown chunk, let save_pdf() use automatically.
code: rb$dims()                                  # list(width, height) in inches #> $width #> [1] 30 #>  #> $height #> [1] 18.3 rb$dims(cell_width = 0.4, cell_height = 0.4, extra_width = 4) #> $width #> [1] 44 #>  #> $height #> [1] 24.4

title: Saving, sizing & interactivity
text: save_pdf() sizes figure dims() (unless pass width/height) uses cairo_pdf default, keeps hyphens hyphens (falling back pdf() platform lacks cairo). background transparent default.
code: p <- rb$plot() rb$save_pdf(p, "rainbow.pdf")                  # auto-sized, cairo device rb$save_pdf(p, "rainbow.pdf", device = "pdf")  # base pdf device (e.g. Windows) rb$save_pdf(p, "rainbow.pdf", bg = "white")    # opaque background  # reserve room for the legend in the page size too (off by default): rb$save_pdf(p, "rainbow.pdf", size_include_legend = TRUE)

title: Saving, sizing & interactivity
text: Legends wide metadata can large. place plot legend independently figure, export separately: save_legend_pdf() writes just legend (auto-sized isn’t clipped), drop_legends() returns plot without , extract_legend() returns legend grob composing patchwork / cowplot / grid.
code: rb$save_legend_pdf(p, "legend.pdf")             # the legend only rb$save_pdf(rb$drop_legends(p), "plot.pdf")     # the plot without its legend legend_grob <- rb$extract_legend(p)             # or compose it yourself

title: Saving, sizing & interactivity
text: plot() carries sample, target, haplotype abundance values extra aesthetics, plotly::ggplotly() surfaces hover:
code: p <- rb$plot() plotly::ggplotly(p)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Nicholas Hathaway. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hathaway N (2026). HaplotypeRainbows: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots. R package version 2.0.0, https://github.com/nickjhathaway/HaplotypeRainbows.
code: @Manual{,   title = {HaplotypeRainbows: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots},   author = {Nicholas Hathaway},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 2.0.0},   url = {https://github.com/nickjhathaway/HaplotypeRainbows}, }

title: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots
text: version v2.0.0 collection tools R create haplotype “rainbows” targeted amplicon (microhaplotype) sequencing data. Unlike SNP barcodes, binary, plots handle arbitrary within-sample haplotype composition. Columns targets, rows samples, cell shows within-sample relative abundance haplotype, coloured dominant haplotype hue rotates across targets — producing characteristic “rainbow”. v2.0.0 breaking change. old standalone functions (prepForRainbow, genRainbowHapPlotObj, …) replaced single R6 class, HaplotypeRainbow, carries column mapping set . Column defaults now follow PMO convention rather SeekDeep.

title: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots
text: 📖 Full documentation, function reference, gallery worked examples live package website: https://nickjhathaway.github.io/HaplotypeRainbows/ Articles cover getting started, colours & plot styles, sample & target metadata, clustering & splitting plots, saving & interactivity.

title: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots
code: # most recent release devtools::install_github('nickjhathaway/HaplotypeRainbows')  # development branch devtools::install_github('nickjhathaway/HaplotypeRainbows@develop')

title: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots
text: tools need minimum 4 columns: Sample name Target / locus name within-target haplotype identifier within-sample relative abundance (raw counts fine — normalised internally) column defaults follow Portable Microhaplotype Object (PMO) convention: can override constructing object.

title: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots
text: Everything runs HaplotypeRainbow R6 class. Transforming methods (prep(), sort_by_clustering(), set_sample_order(), sort_alphabetical()) mutate object return , can chained; plot() returns ggplot object can print, ggsave() hand plotly::ggplotly(). data already uses PMO column names, mapping needed: colours meaning within column (colour = haplotype target), across columns. dominant-haplotype hue steps across targets period (default 11) create rainbow.
code: library(HaplotypeRainbows)  # load example data (uses the older SeekDeep column names) data("pfisolateExample")  # construct with your column mapping (only needed once) rb <- HaplotypeRainbow$new(   pfIsosHeomeV1,   sample_col    = "s_Sample",   target_col    = "p_name",   popuid_col    = "h_popUID",   rel_abund_col = "c_AveragedFrac" )  # prep + plot rb$prep(sort = "population_rank") rb$plot() rb <- HaplotypeRainbow$new(my_pmo_allele_table)   # or haplotype_rainbow(my_pmo_allele_table) rb$prep()$plot()

title: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots
text: Order samples similar samples (haplotype sharing) sit next using hierarchical clustering (ward.D2): ordering options:
code: rb$prep(sort = "population_rank")$sort_by_clustering() rb$plot() rb$sort_by_clustering(by_major_allele = TRUE)   # cluster on the major allele only rb$set_sample_order(c("3D7", "HB3", "..."))      # explicit order rb$sort_alphabetical()                            # default

title: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots
text: Supply palette plot():

title: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots
text: prep(sort = ...) controls haplotypes ordered within cell: "population_rank" — order haplotypes population rank (default) "within_sample_freq" — order haplotypes within-sample fraction

title: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots
text: Instead rotating rainbow, prep_shade() colours target shades per-target base colour. plot() automatically uses shade colours:
code: rb$prep_shade(min_pop_size = 1) rb$plot()

title: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots
text: Target names (x) sample names (y) shown default; toggle either (plot keeps tight margins leftover ticks):
code: rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE)                        # hide target names rb$plot(x_axis_labels = FALSE, y_axis_labels = FALSE) # hide both (e.g. many samples)

title: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots
text: Interactive tooltips: plot() carries sample, target, haplotype abundance values extra aesthetics plotly::ggplotly() shows hover. Migrating v1.x: class methods map onto old functions (prep() → prepForRainbow*, plot() → genRainbowHapPlotObj*, sort_by_clustering() → resort_prepped_samples_by_clustering).

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
text: R6 class building haplotype "rainbow" plots. class carries mapping four data columns (sample, target, haplotype identifier relative counts) supplied . Methods transform state (prep(), sort_by_clustering(), set_sample_order(), sort_alphabetical()) mutate object return invisibly, can chained; plot() returns ggplot2::ggplot object. Column defaults follow Portable Microhaplotype Object (PMO) convention: library_sample_name, target_name, seq reads.

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
text: HaplotypeRainbow$new() HaplotypeRainbow$prep() HaplotypeRainbow$prep_shade() HaplotypeRainbow$sort_by_clustering() HaplotypeRainbow$get_hclust() HaplotypeRainbow$get_dendrogram() HaplotypeRainbow$cluster_groups() HaplotypeRainbow$set_sample_order() HaplotypeRainbow$sort_alphabetical() HaplotypeRainbow$set_sample_meta() HaplotypeRainbow$set_target_meta() HaplotypeRainbow$get_sample_meta() HaplotypeRainbow$get_target_meta() HaplotypeRainbow$sort_samples_by_meta() HaplotypeRainbow$sort_targets_by_meta() HaplotypeRainbow$set_target_order() HaplotypeRainbow$plot() HaplotypeRainbow$get_rank_colors() HaplotypeRainbow$add_sample_metadata() HaplotypeRainbow$add_target_annotation() HaplotypeRainbow$dims() HaplotypeRainbow$save_pdf() HaplotypeRainbow$drop_legends() HaplotypeRainbow$extract_legend() HaplotypeRainbow$save_legend_pdf() HaplotypeRainbow$export_groups_pdf() HaplotypeRainbow$add_cluster_gaps() HaplotypeRainbow$add_cluster_bands() HaplotypeRainbow$get_prepped() HaplotypeRainbow$print() HaplotypeRainbow$clone()

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
text: Create new HaplotypeRainbow.

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
code: HaplotypeRainbow$new(   data,   sample_col = "library_sample_name",   target_col = "target_name",   popuid_col = "seq",   rel_abund_col = "reads" )

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
text: data data frame / tibble one row per (sample, target, haplotype). sample_col Name sample identifier column. target_col Name target / locus column. popuid_col Name within-target haplotype identifier column. rel_abund_col Name relative counts column (raw counts fine; normalised within-sample/target fractions internally).

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
text: Prep data rainbow plot (haplotype colours rotate across targets). Stores prepped table internally.

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
code: HaplotypeRainbow$prep(   sort = c("population_rank", "within_sample_freq"),   min_pop_size = 1,   color_period = 11,   bar_height = 0.8,   mark_invariant = FALSE )

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
text: sort Haplotype ordering within cell: "population_rank" (order population rank) "within_sample_freq" (order within-sample fraction). min_pop_size Drop targets fewer many unique haplotypes. color_period Number hue steps rotate across targets. bar_height Height full stacked bar per sample; < 1 leaves gap. mark_invariant Flag single-haplotype (invariant) targets plot(rank_colors = TRUE) can colour separately (black default).

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
text: object, invisibly (chainable).

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
text: min_pop_size Drop targets fewer many unique haplotypes. bar_height Height full stacked bar per sample; < 1 leaves gap. base_colors Base colours generate per-target shades .

code: HaplotypeRainbow$plot(   style = NULL,   colors = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "Spectral"),   color_col = "popidFracLogColor",   shade_col = "h_color_byFreq_mod",   rank_colors = FALSE,   rank_palette = colorPalette_12,   invariant_color = "#000000",   rank_legend_title = "Microhaplotype Rank",   x_axis_labels = TRUE,   y_axis_labels = TRUE )

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
text: style "rainbow" (gradient rotating hues) "shade" (identity colours shade prep). Defaults "shade" data prepped sort = "shade", otherwise "rainbow". colors Colour ramp rainbow style. Length match color_period. color_col Column map fill rainbow style ("popidFracLogColor" "popidFracRegColor"). shade_col Identity-colour column shade style. rank_colors Colour haplotypes discrete rank (legend) instead continuous rainbow gradient. ranks palette colours, colour ramp interpolated palette (warning); invariant targets (see prep(mark_invariant = TRUE)) get invariant_color. Replaces old harvest-fills--ggplot_build recipe. rank_palette Colours haplotype ranks 1, 2, ... (rank colouring); ranks colours, ramp interpolated . invariant_color Colour invariant (single-haplotype) targets. rank_legend_title Legend title used rank_colors. x_axis_labels Show target names x-axis. y_axis_labels Show sample names y-axis.

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
text: p ggplot returned plot(). cols Metadata columns draw (default: sample-metadata slot), one band , order outward plot. side "left" "right" rainbow. width Band width cell units (1 = one rainbow cell wide). height Band height fraction cell (1 = full cell, centred). gap Gap bands cell units (0 = flush). plot_gap Gap cell units rainbow cells first band (0 = flush plot). colors Optional named list (per column) value -> colour override auto-assigned colours. na_color Fill samples missing metadata value. legend Draw colour legend band. legend_ncol, legend_nrow Number columns / rows band legends, keep tall legends page. scalar applies every band; named vector (e.g. c(country = 3)) sets per column. level_order Optional named list giving legend/level order column (e.g. list(country = c("Uganda", "Kenya"))); unlisted values appended sorted order. labels Draw band (column-name) labels x-axis. Independent target_labels, band labels can stay target names . target_labels Keep target names x-axis. border Border colour band rectangles.

Independent sample_labels. sample_labels Keep sample names y-axis. border Border colour band rectangles.

code: HaplotypeRainbow$export_groups_pdf(   file,   plot_fun,   by = c("cluster", "meta"),   k = NULL,   h = NULL,   meta_col = NULL,   device = c("cairo", "pdf"),   combine = TRUE,   align_targets = TRUE,   size_include_legend = FALSE,   bg = "transparent",   ... )

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
text: file Output PDF path (, combine = FALSE, template whose .pdf suffixed group label). plot_fun function one argument: receives per-group HaplotypeRainbow (prepped data filtered group) returns ggplot. e.g. function(sub) sub$plot(). Group "cluster" (needs prior sort_by_clustering() k/h) "meta" (needs set_sample_meta() meta_col). k, h Cluster cut (= "cluster"). meta_col Sample-metadata column group (= "meta"). device "cairo" (default) "pdf"; cairo falls back pdf() platform lacks cairo support. combine Combine per-group pages one PDF (needs qpdf); FALSE, write separate file per group. align_targets Pad sample-name labels common width (mono font) plot body / targets line position every page. Default TRUE. size_include_legend Passed dims() per-group sizing. bg Device background colour (default "transparent"). ... Passed graphics device.

title: HaplotypeRainbow — HaplotypeRainbow
text: p ggplot returned plot(). k Number cluster groups (passed cluster_groups()). h Height cut tree (alternative k). colors Colours alternate (translucent default rainbow shows ). extend_left, extend_right Extend bands (cell units) cover sidebar annotation strip. expand Push band edges many cells past plotting cells sides, widening panel bands visible margin strips (overlay alone can hard see dense cell grid). border Border colour bands (default none). visible colour draws line around cluster block.

what: HaplotypeRainbows: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots — HaplotypeRainbows-package
title: HaplotypeRainbows: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots — HaplotypeRainbows-package
text: Creates haplotype "rainbow" plots targeted amplicon (microhaplotype) sequencing data. Given table samples, targets, within-target haplotype identifiers relative counts, builds grid columns targets rows samples, cell showing within-sample haplotype composition. Haplotype colours rotate across targets produce characteristic rainbow. package exposes single R6 class, HaplotypeRainbow, carries column mapping set . Column defaults follow Portable Microhaplotype Object (PMO) convention.

title: HaplotypeRainbows: Creates Haplotype Rainbow Plots — HaplotypeRainbows-package
text: Maintainer: Nicholas Hathaway nickjhathaway@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Nicholas Hathaway nickjhathaway@gmail.com (ORCID)

what: Example Plasmodium falciparum isolate haplotype table (SeekDeep column names) — pfIsosHeomeV1
title: Example Plasmodium falciparum isolate haplotype table (SeekDeep column names) — pfIsosHeomeV1
text: Microhaplotype calls Plasmodium falciparum lab isolates mixtures, one row per (sample, target, haplotype). Uses historical SeekDeep column names; pass names explicitly HaplotypeRainbow since class now defaults PMO convention.

title: Example Plasmodium falciparum isolate haplotype table (SeekDeep column names) — pfIsosHeomeV1
text: tibble 7,611 rows 4 columns: s_Sample Sample identifier. p_name Target / locus name. h_popUID Within-target haplotype (population) identifier. c_AveragedFrac Within-sample relative abundance haplotype.

text: data frame 61 rows (one per sample): sample Sample identifier (matches s_Sample). collection_date Collection date, known. country Country origin. site Collection site. collection_year Collection year, known. region Geographic region. subRegion Geographic sub-region. secondaryRegion Broader (continent-level) region.

title: Example target metadata for pfIsosHeomeV1 — pfIsosHeomeV1_targetMeta
text: data frame 100 rows (one per target): target_name Target / locus name (matches p_name). gene_id Overlapping gene identifier. gene_description Gene description. group Whether target included excluded ("include"/"exclude"). class Target class ("Diversity"/"Drug"). chrom Chromosome.
